p.seg: Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting:

Let present and future know that I have given, granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the whole land of Maybole, which Duncan son of Gilbert gave to them, by the correct boundaries contained in the charter of the same Duncan; to be held in pure and pertpetual alms with all easements, and other things justly pertaining to the aforesaid lands, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid Dunca made thereupon for the aforesaid monks bears witness, saving our service.

As witnesses: William, bishop of St Andrews; William del Bois, chancellor; Earl Patrick; Earl Malcolm of Fife; William Comyn, earl of Buchan; Aland son of Roland, constable; Philip de Mowbray; Walter Oliphant; Adam son of Gilbert; Ingram de Balliol; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; Walter of Lindsay; John of Maxwell.
At Edinburgh, on the second day of October.
